items.title: Fire risks and ugly designs are stalling EV charger adoption
items.summary: From Seoul to New York City, residents are fighting charging infrastructure over safety, aesthetics, and crowding.

items.title: From Chile to the Philippines, meet the people pushing back on AI
items.summary: Individuals and communities are resisting the demands and practices of Big Tech's AI infrastructure — such as data centers and digital labor — due to their environmental and social costs.

items.title: The Gulf was Silicon Valley’s bet on the future. Trump has put it in the crosshairs
items.summary: The same choke points that made the Gulf the world’s energy crossroads now threaten its role as the nerve center of the AI age.

items.title: The stark divide in the UAE and India war info systems
items.summary: My experience of the tech platforms, media content, and official messaging of the war in two cities.

items.title: Africa pours $2 billion into controversial Chinese surveillance tech
items.summary: A new study shows Chinese firms and banks are behind much of the continent’s AI-powered monitoring infrastructure.

items.title: “It feels like Squid Game”: China’s workers scramble to keep up in the AI race
items.summary: Employees are rushing to learn new tools as layoffs and automation fuel widespread AI anxiety.

items.title: China is mobilizing thousands of one-person AI startups
items.summary: Under a national AI push, local governments are turning coworking space and data centers into AI incubators.

items.title: Why refusing AI is a fight for the soul
items.summary: Author Thomas Dekeyser explains why modern resistance to Big Tech is a deeply sane response to a narrow vision of humanity.

items.title: Can Africa succeed where India failed with the $40 smartphone?
items.summary: Mobile operators and global groups are testing ultracheap 4G phones across six African countries, hoping to close the continent’s device affordability gap.

items.title: Western AI models “fail spectacularly” in farms and forests abroad
items.summary: Big Tech’s AI tools trained on Western data often can’t recognize local crops, forests, or farming conditions without adaptation to local environments.

items.title: The Gulf war is reshaping how Asia works
items.summary: Signs reading 'out of stock' at a gas station amid rising petrol prices in Manila, Philippines on March 9, 2026.

items.title: The Gulf built oil pipelines to avoid Hormuz. It’s now doing the same for data
items.summary: Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and the UAE are financing competing data corridors through Syria, Iraq, and East Africa to bypass the two maritime choke points that threaten their digital connectivity.
